title: Roster
description: An army list imported from an external list-builder, resolved onto 40kdc entity IDs. Lenient: unresolved entries are retained with their raw name and candidate suggestions rather than dropped, and a diagnostics block summarises the import. Contains only permitted facts (entity IDs, display names, model counts, points, keywords) — never reproduced rules or ability text.

source.description: Provenance of the imported list. `format` identifies which adapter parsed the source payload; new adapters extend this enum.

generated_by.description: The `generatedBy` field reported by the source payload (e.g. 'List Forge', 'https://newrecruit.eu'), if present.

faction_id.description: Resolved primary faction; null when the source faction could not be matched.

detachments.description: Resolved detachments. 11e lists may field several detachments under a detachment-point cap; pre-11e lists carry zero or one. Order is load-bearing (source/parse order). Empty when the source declared no detachment.

battle_size.description: Resolved battle size; null when the source value could not be mapped.

points.description: Point totals. `total_reported` (from the source payload) and `total_computed` (summed from this roster's units) are kept distinct and never reconciled — a mismatch is surfaced as a diagnostic.

declared_limit.description: Points ceiling parsed from the battle size (e.g. 2000), if known.

detachment_cap.description: 11e detachment-point budget derived from the battle size (strike-force 3, incursion 2); null when the battle size is unknown. The sum of each detachment's `dp_cost` is meant to fit within this cap.

total_reported.description: Total points as reported by the source roster's cost block.

total_computed.description: Sum of this roster's per-unit point values.

roster-resolved-ref.description: A reference to a 40kdc entity that may or may not have resolved. Retains the source's raw name so the import is lossless even on a miss.

id.description: Resolved entity id, or null when no match was found.

raw_name.description: The display name exactly as it appeared in the source payload.

candidates.description: Best-guess alternatives offered when resolution failed (empty when resolved or when no near-matches exist).

roster-detachment.description: One detachment selected on the roster, paired with its resolved detachment-point cost.

dp_cost.description: Detachment-point cost (1–3) looked up from the resolved detachment entity's `detachment_points`. Null when the detachment is unresolved or the entity records no cost.

points.description: Per-unit point value from the source, or null when absent.

enhancement.description: Attached enhancement, or null when none.

enhancement_points.description: Points cost of the attached enhancement when the source reported one. Null when there is no enhancement or when the source didn't carry a cost line for it. Lets a Roster round-trip cleanly through formats that print enhancements as a separate `+N pts` line.

wargear.description: Weapons/wargear selected on the unit. Always lists the raw name and count; `ref.id` is null when the weapon could not be matched.

oneOf.description: Inferred leader→bodyguard attachment. Always provisional: the source format does not encode attachment unambiguously, so this is a heuristic match against leader-attachment data.

roster-diagnostics.description: A summary of what resolved and what did not during the import.
